judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently reach the same correct diagnosis, root cause, timeline, and rebuttal of the flaky-model theory with nearly identical supporting evidence; they are comparable in substance and completeness.

report_1.evidence: The defect is in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml` — specifically line 2, `expected_label_field: label`. ... But every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` stores its gold answer under the key `gold_label`, not `label`

report_2.evidence: The root cause is a stale configuration value left behind by an incomplete schema migration... `pipeline/score_batch.py` determines which JSON field holds the gold label by reading `config["expected_label_field"]` from **`config/scoring_config.yaml`**, which currently contains `expected_label_field: label`. But `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` no longer has a `"label"` key on any record — every record uses `"gold_label"`

report_1.evidence: It began 2026-06-17, introduced by commit `53551e8...`, "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"... That commit's diff ... renamed every record's key in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label` ... This is corroborated independently by `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv`: accuracy sits at 0.90–0.92 every night through 2026-06-16, then drops to exactly `0.00` on 2026-06-17

report_2.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, and was introduced by commit `53551e8` — `chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)`... The evidence lines up exactly: `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows 0.90–0.92 every day through 2026-06-16, then `0.00` starting precisely 2026-06-17

report_1.evidence: The earlier investigation's method was structurally incapable of finding this: it exclusively used `pipeline/score_one.py`, which — as I confirmed by reading its source — never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never loads `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`, and never compares anything to a gold label; it just prints `predict_category(...)` for one ticket.

report_2.evidence: The prior note re-ran tickets through `pipeline/score_one.py`. I read that file: it calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the label — it never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads `gold_label`, and never performs any comparison at all.

report_1.evidence: Even in a hypothetical where model-switching existed, it couldn't fix this: the defect is entirely in how `score_batch.py` looks up the gold label, which is completely independent of which classifier produced the prediction. Rolling back "the model" leaves `expected_label_field: label` and the `gold_label`-keyed fixture untouched, so accuracy would stay at exactly `0.00`

report_2.evidence: If the team acts on this, at best it's a costly no-op that changes nothing about `expected_label_field`, so the nightly accuracy will keep logging `0.00` indefinitely; at worst it burns time standing up/pointing at infrastructure that doesn't exist for a problem it can't touch, while the real one-line config defect remains live in `config/scoring_config.yaml`
